records.ety: [Pref. dis- + band: cf. OF. desbander, F. dbander, to unbind, unbend. See Band, and cf. Disbend, Disbind.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disbanded; p. pr. & vb. n. Disbanding.]

defs: To loose the bands of; to set free; to disunite; to scatter; to disperse; to break up the organization of; especially, to dismiss from military service; as, to disband an army.
quotes: They disbanded themselves and returned, every man to his own dwelling.
And therefore . . . she ought to be disbanded.

defs: To become separated, broken up, dissolved, or scattered; especially, to quit military service by breaking up organization.
quotes: When both rocks and all things shall disband.
Human society would in a short space disband.
